articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: New Reforms Aim to Revive Zimbabwe's Agriculture Sector
more.description: [263Chat] Government has unveiled sweeping reforms aimed at reducing the cost of doing business in agriculture,a sector it says remains central to the country's economic growth and food security.

more.title: Zimbabwe to play Nigeria in 4-nations tournament in London
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe will next month compete in the Unity Cup 2026 in London — the first time Zimbabwe have played in England in a tournament of this nature. more.title: Highlanders suspends CEO over Botswana camp ‘financial irregularities’
more.description: BULAWAYO – Highlanders have suspended chief executive officer Denzil Mnkandla over alleged financial mismanagement linked to the club’s recent tour of Botswana, while two other senior officials are facing mounting pressure over the same issue. more.title: Iran and US reach ceasefire agreement, easing Middle East tensions
more.description: The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire barely an hour before President Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate the country, triggering global relief alongside apprehension. more.title: Rapper Lil Tjay Arrested After Shooting Incident Involving Offset at Florida Casino
more.description: Florida, United States — American rapper Lil Tjay was arrested on Monday night following an altercation outside the Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino, where fellow rapper Offset sustained a gunshot wound. Authorities confirmed that the 24-year-old artist was taken into custody and booked at Broward County Jail on a misdemeanour charge of disorderly conduct […]

more.title: Allegations swirl on Chivayo cross‑border gold and cash network
more.description: Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo has become the subject of renewed scrutiny following explosive allegations circulating on social media claiming he is at the centre of a sophisticated cross‑border financial network involving gold movements and cash transfers across Africa and Europe. more.title: Free Wi-Fi hotspots for Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe is set to mark its 46th Independence Anniversary with the nationwide rollout of free public Wi-Fi hotspots, a government initiative aimed at expanding digital access and fostering innovation among young people.
